commitmentAction: Next class, after every pair-talk, cold-call one non-volunteer before taking hands so the silent half of the room gets a turn at reasoning out loud.

analysis.subject: Mathematics
analysis.photo_analysis: The classroom photo shows students seated in 5 groups of 4, materials (fraction strips and worksheets) evenly distributed on each table, and a whiteboard with the objective written top-left.

indicators.name: Lesson Goal Clarity
indicators.evidence: Teacher opened with the objective 'By the end of class you'll find an equivalent fraction for any fraction I give you' and tied it to the prior lesson on halves.

indicators.name: Fidelity to LP Steps
indicators.evidence: Followed the connect–explain–model–guided–independent arc; minor logistical pause during material handout.

indicators.name: Materials Use
indicators.evidence: Fraction strips and pizza-slice cutouts were introduced right when the doubling rule was named — the material carried the abstraction.

indicators.name: Time Management
indicators.evidence: Pacing broadly on-plan; 4 minutes of independent work extended by ~90 seconds because of a sit-with-stuck-student move.

indicators.name: Explanation & Modeling
indicators.evidence: Modeled 1/2 = 2/4 on the board with a labelled diagram before generalizing to the ×n rule.

indicators.name: Questioning Technique
indicators.evidence: Recurrent 'How did you figure that out?' prompt after every right answer — reasoning, not the answer, became the currency.

indicators.name: Guided Practice
indicators.evidence: Pair-talk on 'what equals half when the pizza is 8 pieces' gave the class a rehearsal before independent work.

indicators.name: Differentiation
indicators.evidence: One quiet sit-with-stuck-student move; no planned extension task for early finishers.

indicators.name: Monitoring Understanding
indicators.evidence: Circulated during independent work and paused to check three notebooks; no aggregate check-in.

indicators.name: Behavioral Climate
indicators.evidence: Orderly, teacher used 'Sit down everyone' calmly at the transition; students settled within ~10 seconds.

indicators.name: Routines & Transitions
indicators.evidence: Transition into groups was signalled and orderly; material handout was smooth after brief queue at the corner.

indicators.name: Respectful Interactions
indicators.evidence: Frequent praise tied to specific behaviour ('Brilliant reasoning'); teacher invited student explanations by name.

indicators.name: Safety & Inclusiveness
indicators.evidence: Called on a mix of volunteers and non-volunteers; one very quiet student was not brought in.

indicators.name: Cognitive Engagement
indicators.evidence: Students articulated 'we doubled, so we doubled' — a student-generated statement of the equivalence rule.

indicators.name: Participation
indicators.evidence: About 60% of the class contributed at least one verbal turn; the same three hands led whole-class shares.

indicators.name: Collaboration
indicators.evidence: Pair-talk was productive; groups formed only for material handout, not for cross-checking answers.

indicators.name: Use of Materials
indicators.evidence: Only ~half the groups actually manipulated the fraction strips; some used them as bookmarks.

indicators.name: Formative Checks
indicators.evidence: No aggregate check (mini-whiteboard, thumbs, cold call for a rule) between guided and independent.

indicators.name: Quality of Feedback
indicators.evidence: Feedback was specific and process-oriented on the moves that were checked ('You multiplied both by 5 — that's the key idea').

indicators.name: Accuracy of Marking
indicators.evidence: Only three notebooks reached during circulation; two of them contained an equivalent-fraction error that went unnoticed.

indicators.name: Responsive Instruction
indicators.evidence: No mid-lesson adjustment based on the check that didn't happen; plan proceeded as written.

analysis.executive_summary: A high-quality Class 6 maths lesson on equivalent fractions. The teacher used the "how did you figure that out?" prompt consistently, turning every right answer into a reasoning moment. The lesson would deepen further by cold-calling the silent half of the class and adding one aggregate formative check between guided and independent work.
